seoTitle: Grants Beach vs Granville — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Grants Beach and Granville. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Grants Beach vs Granville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Grants Beach and Granville.
heading: Grants Beach vs Granville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Grants Beach, New South Wales and Granville, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Granville has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Grants Beach (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Grants Beach or Granville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Grants Beach has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Granville scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Grants Beach scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Granville scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Grants Beach or Granville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Granville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
